Local-first browsing analytics + one-click tab organizer for Chrome. Replace your new tab, see where your hours actually go.

See where your time actually goes online.

SiteLogs is a Chrome extension that quietly records how long you spend
on each website — locally, on your machine. Replaces your new tab with
a personal dashboard, organizes 47 stale tabs in one click, and never
sends a byte anywhere.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
✦ What it does
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

⏱ Honest time tracking — counts active focus minutes, pauses on idle.
   Not "tab open duration". Per-URL, per-domain, per-category.

🏠 Custom new tab dashboard — every new tab opens your personal stats,
   pinned shortcuts, hot sites, and the 24×7 heatmap. No Google search
   bar, no sponsored links.

📌 Pin frequently-used sites — one-click access from the new tab.

🗂 One-click tab cleanup — close duplicate URLs, group remaining tabs
   by domain into Chrome's native colored tab groups. Save the current
   window as a named Session, restore it later.

📈 Period-over-period analysis — today vs your average Tuesday, this
   week vs last week, new domains vs lapsed.

🎯 Focus blocks (≥10 min continuous reading) auto-detected. Daily
   focus budgets per category with progress bars.

🏷 Tags + custom rules — 200+ entry built-in dictionary maps domains
   to 13 categories; override with your own domain or regex rules.

🌏 EN / 中文 — fully bilingual, switch in Settings.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
✦ Privacy by design
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

100% local-first. Your browsing data lives in your browser's IndexedDB
on this device — never uploaded, never indexed by anyone else. We have
no server. We can't see what you visit, even subpoenaed.

The only outbound calls are Google Fonts CSS (will self-host) and
license-key activation when you pay.

No telemetry. No analytics. No external tracking. No login required.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
✦ Pricing
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

7-day free trial — full features, no credit card up front.
$15 lifetime — one-time, no subscription.
$10 early-bird — first 100 customers.
30-day money-back — no questions asked.

Activate on up to 5 devices with a single license key.

Payments handled by Paddle (Merchant of Record).

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
✦ Use cases
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

👩‍💻 Indie makers — split "tools" from "leisure" to see your real
   builder hours.
🧠 Knowledge workers — surface focus blocks vs context-switch tax.
🎓 Students & researchers — the 24×7 heatmap shows what the diary
   won't admit.

SurfMate lets users highlight, save, and share annotated web content for streamlined research and collaboration.

**SurfMate - Simplify Research, Sharing & Collaboration**

WHY SurfMate?
SurfMate is created to tackle a common frustration faced by students, professionals, and teams—managing, annotating, and sharing online research efficiently. Traditional bookmarks and browsing history are limited. It's hard to remember why a page was saved, find specific info later, or share insights easily with others.

THE PROBLEM

* Lost context: Bookmarks don’t capture why the link mattered
* Time-consuming sharing: You need to manually summarise links when sharing with colleagues
* Inefficient revisiting: Difficult to locate quotes, numbers, and insights during documentation, presentations or when you need to find it the most
* Overwhelmed by tabs: Browsers slow down with too many tabs, research becomes chaotic. You keep most tabs open due to fear of missing out important information

THE SOLUTION
SurfMate solves this by enabling you to quickly save content along with the URL, organise it automatically, and share research effortlessly—with full context preserved. When a user opens the shared link, he/she can view annotations highlighted effortlessly on the page. Group by domain & time along with search enables you to quickly find information you saved.

KEY FEATURES

* Instant Annotations: Highlight and save important text from any webpage
* Automatic Organisation: Links and notes grouped by time and domain
* Context-Rich Sharing: Shared links open with highlights visible instantly
* Multi-Annotate: Save multiple highlights on one page
* Rapid Retrieval: Quickly search saved insights

HOW IT WORKS (IN 3 SIMPLE STEPS)

1. Save & Annotate: Select text → right-click → Save to SurfMate
2. Organise & Manage: Click SurfMate icon to view links grouped by time and domain
3. Share Easily: Copy annotated link and share with others—no need to summarise

WHY BOOKMARKS & HISTORY FALL SHORT

* Bookmarks don’t retain context or key info
* History is messy and hard to navigate
* Neither lets you annotate, search highlights, or share insights easily

INSTALL SurfMate TODAY
Take control of your research and important links. Save what matters. Share what counts. SurfMate is your Chrome companion to boost productivity, preserve context, and make collaboration effortless.
Spotlight-style launcher for browser history, bookmarks, and open tabs

Xun (寻) is a fast, keyboard-driven launcher that lets you jump to anything in your browser — open tabs, bookmarks, and history — from a single overlay.

Press Ctrl+K (Cmd+K on Mac) to open. Start typing and results appear instantly, ranked by frecency (frequency + recency). No server, no account, everything runs locally.

Features:
- Instantly find tabs, bookmarks, and history from one place
- Frecency ranking — recently and frequently visited pages rise to the top
- Ghost text auto-completion from your browsing history
- Prefix filters (t for tabs, b for bookmarks, h for history)
- Custom plugins — filter by URL pattern or open parameterized URLs
- Built-in /compute calculator and /translate (EN↔中文)
- New tab page with launcher auto-focused
- Configurable keyboard shortcut
- Config sync across browsers via remote server (optional)
- Catppuccin Mocha dark theme

Xun is open source, privacy-first, and works entirely offline. No data leaves your browser unless you enable optional config sync.

寻 means "to seek" in Chinese.
Have I visited this page before?

By installing this extension, you will be able to know how many times you've visited the page you are currently looking at by simply taking a look at your browser history and show you the number in of visits in the badge.

Easily switch between recent tabs with keyboard shortcut Ctrl+E.

New in 1.1: A close tab button

Tezbar - Simple Fast Omnibar

Features:
- Search current tabs
- Search historic tabs
- Custom site search for: 
  1. Wikipedia
  2. Google Maps
  3. Perplexity
  4. YouTube

Inspired by Arc Browser Search Bar
Salesforce OrgPilot: An Extension for environment watermarks and fast navigation in Salesforce orgs.

Salesforce OrgPilot
A browser extension (Manifest V3) for Chrome and Firefox that provides environment identification watermarks and quick navigation for Salesforce orgs.

Features
Environment Watermark
Automatically displays a watermark identifying whether you're in Production or a Sandbox (showing the sandbox name). The watermark appears on page load so you can verify your environment at a glance, then disappears on your first click or keypress to stay out of the way. It reappears on the next page load.

Customizable via the options page:

Setting	Default	Range
Font size	120px	50-300px
Opacity	20%	5-50%
Production color	#ff4444 (red)	Any hex color
Sandbox color	#006600 (green)	Any hex color
Position	Diagonal	Diagonal, Top Left, Top Right, Top Center, Center
Omnibox Quick Navigation
Type sf in the browser address bar followed by a target to quickly navigate anywhere in your Salesforce org. Single-word commands default to production; prefix with a sandbox name to target a sandbox.

sf <target>                    # goes to production (current tab)
sf <sandbox> <target>          # goes to a sandbox (current tab)
sf *<sandbox> <target>         # opens in a new tab
sf **<sandbox> <target>        # opens in a new window
Environments:

Single word defaults to production (e.g., sf cases → prod cases)
Two words: first word is the sandbox name (e.g., sf dev cases → dev sandbox cases)
Tab/Window behavior:

No prefix — navigates in the current tab (default)
* prefix — opens in a new tab (e.g., sf *dev cases)
** prefix — opens in a new window (e.g., sf **dev cases)
Targets:

Target	Destination
admin	Setup Home
flow / flows	Flow Builder
login	Base URL / login page for the org
copy	Current page in another environment
sandbox	Sandbox creation page
devops	DevOps Center (production only)
Custom target name	User-defined path (see Custom Targets)
Any object name (e.g., account, contact)	Recent list view for that object
15 or 18-character Salesforce ID	Direct record navigation
Examples:

sf account            → Production Account list
sf flow               → Production Flow Builder
sf admin              → Production Setup Home
sf 001xx000003DGbY    → Navigate directly to a production record
sf login              → Production base URL
sf dev login          → Dev sandbox base URL
sf dive copy          → Open current page in dive sandbox
sf copy               → Open current page in production
sf dev flow           → Dev sandbox Flow Builder
sf qa admin           → QA sandbox Setup Home
sf dev 001xx000003DGbY   → Navigate to a record in dev sandbox
sf *prod admin           → Production Setup Home in a new tab
sf **dev cases           → Dev sandbox cases in a new window
sf users                 → Custom target "users" in production
sf dev users             → Custom target "users" in dev sandbox
Popup Dashboard
Click the extension icon to open a quick-access popup with:

Environment selector — Switch between Production and your configured sandboxes with one click. The selected environment is remembered across popup opens.
Navigate buttons — Admin, Flows, Sandbox, DevOps on the first row; Login and Copy on the second. Copy takes the current tab's page and opens it in the selected environment.
Custom targets — Any custom targets you've configured appear in their own section.
Recent Pages — Shows the last 5 Salesforce pages you visited, with relative timestamps ("just now", "5m ago", "2h ago"). Click any entry to navigate back. Respects the open-in dropdown.
Quick Nav — Type a Salesforce ID (15 or 18 characters) or an object name to navigate directly. Includes autocomplete suggestions for standard objects (Account, Contact, Lead, Opportunity, Case, etc.).
Open in dropdown — Choose whether links open in the current tab, a new tab, or a new window.
Clean Up Groups — Merges duplicate tab groups in the current window.
Consolidate — Dropdown lists every tab group that exists in other browser windows. Select one (e.g., PROD, DEV) and click Consolidate to pull all tabs from that group across all windows into the current window. Sandbox tabs in other groups are left untouched.
Configure sandbox names in the options page (comma-separated, e.g. DEV, QA, UAT). They display with whatever casing you enter but URLs are always lowercase.

Tab Grouping by Environment
Automatically organizes Salesforce tabs into color-coded browser tab groups by environment. Production tabs go into a red PROD group, and each sandbox gets its own distinctly colored group (e.g., green DEV, blue QA).

Auto-grouping — Enable in options to automatically group every Salesforce tab as it loads
Manual grouping — Press Alt+Shift+G (Option+Shift+G on Mac) to sort all open Salesforce tabs into groups at once. Works even when auto-grouping is off.
Cross-window consolidation — Use the Consolidate dropdown in the popup to pick any tab group and pull all its tabs from other browser windows into the current one.
Tabs that navigate between environments are automatically moved to the correct group
Non-Salesforce tabs are never affected
Each browser window maintains its own set of groups
Empty groups are automatically removed by the browser
Keyboard Shortcuts
Shortcut	Action
Alt+S (Option+S on Mac)	Open the popup
Alt+Shift+G (Option+Shift+G on Mac)	Group all Salesforce tabs by environment
Alt+Shift+T (Option+Shift+T on Mac)	Pull all Salesforce tabs into current window and group
Remap shortcuts at chrome://extensions/shortcuts.

Installation
Chrome
Clone or download this repository
Open chrome://extensions/ in Chrome
Enable Developer Mode (toggle in the top right)
Click Load unpacked and select this directory
Open the extension's Options page to configure your production URL
Firefox
Clone or download this repository
Open about:debugging#/runtime/this-firefox in Firefox
Click Load Temporary Add-on
Select the manifest.json file from this directory
Open the extension's Options page to configure your production URL
Configuration
Open the extension options page to set:

Production URL — Your Salesforce production domain (e.g., myorg.lightning.force.com). The org name is extracted from the first segment of this URL and used for environment detection and navigation.
Sandbox Names — Comma-separated list of sandbox environment names (e.g., DEV, QA, UAT). These appear as buttons in the popup dashboard.
Tab Grouping — Toggle auto-grouping of Salesforce tabs by environment. Off by default.
Watermark settings — Toggle, font size, opacity, colors, and position.
Custom Targets — Up to 10 custom name/URL pairs. Paste a full Salesforce URL and the path is extracted automatically. Target names support flexible plural matching (site matches sites and vice versa).
All settings sync across browser instances via chrome.storage.sync.

Export / Import Settings
From the options page, you can Export your entire configuration as a sfnav-config.json file for backup or sharing, and Import a previously exported file to restore settings. Import validates the file before applying and reloads the page on success.

How Environment Detection Works
The extension determines the environment from the page hostname:

Production — Hostname starts with orgName. (e.g., myorg.lightning.force.com)
Sandbox — Hostname contains orgName-- (e.g., myorg--dev.sandbox.lightning.force.com), with the sandbox name extracted and displayed as the watermark label
Supported domains:

*.salesforce.com
*.lightning.force.com
*.salesforce-setup.com
Project Structure
├── manifest.json    # Extension configuration (Manifest V3)
├── content.js       # Watermark injection (runs on Salesforce pages)
├── background.js    # Omnibox navigation, tab grouping, and command handler
├── popup.html       # Popup dashboard UI
├── popup.js         # Popup dashboard logic
├── options.html     # Settings page UI
├── options.js       # Settings page logic
└── icons/           # Extension icons (16, 32, 48, 128px)
Development
No build tools required — vanilla JS, HTML, and CSS loaded directly by the browser.

After making changes:

Chrome: Go to chrome://extensions/ and click the reload button on the extension card
Firefox: Go to about:debugging#/runtime/this-firefox and click Reload

全文検索・自動タグ付け・ドメイン別表示で、もっと便利な閲覧履歴体験を

Histraは、Chromeの履歴ページをもっと使いやすくする拡張機能です。

  ■ 全文検索
  URLやタイトルだけでなく、ページ本文の内容でも検索できます。日本語にも対応。「あのページ、なんて名前だっけ？」をすぐに解決します。

  ■ 自動タグ付け
  訪問したサイトをDev・SNS・動画・ショッピングなどのカテゴリに自動分類。履歴をタグでフィルタリングして、目的のページをすばやく見つけられます。

  ■ カスタムタグ
  自分だけのタグを作成して履歴を自由に整理。仕事用・趣味・あとで読むなど、好きな分類で管理できます。

  ■ タイムライン表示
  日付ごとにグループ化された見やすい時系列ビュー。いつ何を見たか、一目で把握できます。

  ■ ドメイン別表示
  サイトごとに訪問回数と最終訪問日を集計。よく使うサイトの利用状況がわかります。

  ■ ダークモード対応
  システム設定に連動して、ライト／ダークを自動切り替え。

  ■ プライバシー重視
  データはすべてブラウザ内（IndexedDB）に保存。外部サーバーへの送信は一切ありません。
Upload file to GoogleDrive and add Scrapbox page

Web上で見つけたファイルを右クリックからGoogleDriveに保存します。
同時にScrapboxのページを作成することで、ファイル管理に役立てます。

Tab Spotlight turns tab chaos into instant clarity. Hit Cmd/Ctrl + K to open a fast, Spotlight-style switcher that helps you jump back to the tabs you already have open, surface pages from your history, and keep your browser lean instead of spawning endless duplicates. It’s built for people who live with too many tabs and want a calmer, quicker way to move through the web.
